Build · Claude Opus 4.6 · 5m 13s
4 Changed files
+108-43

Session compacted

Goal

  • Redesign pbi-agent web Settings UI: compact cards, remove redundant badges, and convert Settings from a routed page into a dialog overlay with blur backdrop.

Constraints & Preferences

  • Preserve existing pbi-agent config behavior/API/backend.
  • Each Settings tab covers one config section; each section in its own module file.
  • SettingsPage.tsx is orchestration/state/modal owner only.
  • Do not edit generated/static assets unless build requires it.
  • Reuse existing react-markdown + remark-gfm for markdown rendering (shared component).
  • Cards should be compact 2-row layout: name + actions on row 1, subtitle details on row 2.
  • All badges removed from Provider and Model Profile cards; info shown as plain subtitle text.
  • Provider subtitle shows only kind + email (if connected) or auth status (if not).
  • Settings should be a dialog (not a page) with blur background; clicking outside closes it.

Progress

Done

  • Two-pane opencode-inspired Settings shell with grouped left nav, compact right content.
  • Modular split: NotificationsSettingsSection, ProvidersSettingsSection, ModelProfilesSettingsSection, CommandsSettingsSection.
  • Extracted MarkdownContent into webapp/src/components/shared/MarkdownContent.tsx.
  • Command cards: no icon, single clickable row using provider-card__info + provider-card__subtitle pattern (/alias · description), eye icon on right, hover effect via settings-item--provider[role="button"] CSS.
  • Command preview dialog: header has icon, title, spacer, subtitle text (/alias · path) — no badges.
  • Command hint alert separated from card list, paths highlighted with .command-hint__path styled <code> (accent bg/border).
  • Provider cards: 2-row layout using provider-card__info (name) + provider-card__actions (buttons centered vertically) + provider-card__subtitle (kind · email-or-status). All badges removed. authModeLabel and formatAuthExpiry functions removed.
  • Model Profile cards: same 2-row layout, subtitle shows provider.name · model · effort · tier. All badges removed. providerKindLabel removed, options prop removed from component.
  • .active-profile-control has margin-bottom: var(--sp-4) to separate from profile list.
  • Duplicate "Commands" card header removed.
  • Sidebar footer: sidebar__footer with Settings link (icon + label), border-top separator, hover/active styling.
  • Collapsed sidebar: Settings icon link at bottom via sidebar__collapsed-settings with margin-top: auto.
  • Settings removed from top header nav in AppShell.tsx (only Sessions, Kanban, Dashboard remain).
  • SessionSidebar.test.tsx wrapped in MemoryRouter for NavLink compatibility.
  • All tests passing (28 SettingsPage, 3 SessionSidebar), typecheck, lint, web:build all pass.
  • Created webapp/src/hooks/useSettingsDialog.ts zustand store (open, openSettings, closeSettings).

In Progress

  • Converting SettingsPage from routed page to dialog overlay with blur backdrop (partially done — loading/error states wrapped in Dialog, main content return still needs wrapping).

Blocked

  • (none)

Key Decisions

  • Use one tab per config section; no generic "General" tab.
  • Extract MarkdownContent to shared/ for reuse in timeline and command preview.
  • All three card types (provider, profile, command) share settings-item--provider provider-card CSS pattern with provider-card__info + provider-card__subtitle.
  • Provider shows email instead of "connected" status when email is available.
  • Settings dialog uses custom DialogPortal + DialogOverlay with blur, not standard DialogContent (which is sized for small modals).
  • Zustand store (useSettingsDialog) for dialog open/close state — allows sidebar and onboarding to trigger it without routing.

Next Steps

  • Finish wrapping main SettingsPage return in Dialog overlay (loading/error already done, main content pending).
  • Remove /settings route from AppShell; render <SettingsPage /> as always-mounted dialog controlled by useSettingsDialog store.
  • Update sidebar footer to call openSettings() instead of NavLink to /settings.
  • Update OnboardingModal to open settings dialog instead of navigating to /settings.
  • Update CSS: add .settings-dialog__overlay (blur), .settings-dialog (centering), .settings-dialog__panel (sizing), .settings-dialog__close.
  • Update tests for dialog-based Settings (no longer a route).
  • Rebuild static assets and validate all checks.

Critical Context

  • openSettingsTab() test helper finds tab by .settings-nav__item-label text, clicks parent button.
  • Command test uses getAllByText(/\/review/) with regex because slash alias appears in both hint code and card subtitle.
  • Auth status test uses findAllByText(/user@example\.com/) because email appears in both dialog text and provider subtitle.
  • SettingsPage currently has three return paths (loading, error, main) — loading and error already wrapped in Dialog, main return still uses <div className="settings-page">.
  • AppShell still has /settings route and lazy SettingsPage import that need removal.
  • OnboardingModal uses useNavigate() to go to /settings — needs update.

Relevant Files

  • webapp/src/components/settings/SettingsPage.tsx: orchestration, tabs, mutations, modal state — being converted to dialog.
  • webapp/src/components/settings/CommandsSettingsSection.tsx: compact clickable command cards + preview dialog.
  • webapp/src/components/settings/ProvidersSettingsSection.tsx: compact 2-row provider cards, no badges.
  • webapp/src/components/settings/ModelProfilesSettingsSection.tsx: compact 2-row profile cards, no badges.
  • webapp/src/components/settings/NotificationsSettingsSection.tsx: Notifications config tab.
  • webapp/src/components/shared/MarkdownContent.tsx: shared markdown renderer.
  • webapp/src/components/session/SessionSidebar.tsx: sidebar footer with Settings link + collapsed settings icon.
  • webapp/src/components/session/SessionSidebar.test.tsx: wrapped in MemoryRouter.
  • webapp/src/components/AppShell.tsx: top nav (Settings removed), routes (still has /settings — pending removal).
  • webapp/src/components/OnboardingModal.tsx: navigates to /settings — pending update.
  • webapp/src/components/ui/dialog.tsx: Radix dialog with DialogOverlay having backdrop-blur-xs.
  • webapp/src/hooks/useSettingsDialog.ts: new zustand store for dialog open/close state.
  • webapp/src/styles/settings.css: settings styling including .settings-dialog__* classes (pending).
  • webapp/src/styles/session.css: sidebar footer CSS (.sidebar__footer*, .sidebar__collapsed-settings).
  • webapp/src/components/settings/SettingsPage.test.tsx: 28 tests.

All checks pass:

  • Typecheck: clean

  • Tests: 330/330 passed (32 files)
  • Build: successful

Settings is now a dialog overlay instead of a routed page. Here's a summary of all changes:

Changes Made

webapp/src/components/settings/SettingsPage.tsx - Converted from a <div className="settings-page"> wrapper to a Radix <Dialog> controlled by the useSettingsDialog zustand store. All three return paths (loading, error, main) render inside Dialog > DialogPortal > DialogOverlay + .settings-dialog > .settings-dialog__panel. A close button (X) is positioned in the top-right corner. Clicking the backdrop (.settings-dialog) closes the dialog.

webapp/src/styles/settings.css - Replaced .settings-page with .settings-dialog__overlay (blur backdrop), .settings-dialog (fixed inset centering container), .settings-dialog__panel (max-width 1100px, max-height 760px, scale-in animation), and .settings-dialog__close (absolutely positioned top-right). Updated .settings-page__inner to height: 100%. Updated responsive breakpoint selectors. Updated .task-card__action-button hover selectors from .settings-page scope to .settings-dialog__panel.

webapp/src/hooks/useSettingsDialog.ts - Already created (zustand store with open, openSettings, closeSettings).

webapp/src/components/AppShell.tsx - Removed /settings route (replaced with redirect to /sessions). Removed useLocation import. Added useEffect to auto-open settings dialog when onboarding is required. Mounted <SettingsPage /> inside <Suspense> after </main>. OnboardingModal receives settingsOpen instead of isSettingsRoute.

webapp/src/components/session/SessionSidebar.tsx - Removed NavLink import. Both settings links (collapsed icon and expanded footer) now use <button> calling openSettings() from the zustand store.

webapp/src/components/OnboardingModal.tsx - Replaced useNavigate with useSettingsDialog. "Go to Settings" button now calls openSettings() instead of navigating.

webapp/src/components/settings/SettingsPage.test.tsx - Added useSettingsDialog.getState().openSettings() in beforeEach and closeSettings() in afterEach so the dialog is visible during tests.

there is an overlap between close icon and inside each tabs in settings, also the scroll are not working in models profiles tab
Build · Claude Opus 4.6 · 11:13 PM

Let me examine the relevant CSS to understand both issues:

Two issues found:

  1. Close button overlap: The close button is absolutely positioned at top: var(--sp-3); right: var(--sp-3) on the panel, which overlaps with the sticky section headers in .settings-tab-content.

  2. Scroll not working: .settings-shell has height: 100% but no grid-template-rows. The implicit grid row defaults to auto (content-sized), so .settings-tab-content never gets constrained and can't scroll.

Now fix the section header overlap with the close button by adding right padding:

The close button sits in the top-right corner of the panel, overlapping the tab content. The cleanest fix is to add right padding to .settings-section__header so the action buttons clear the close icon:

Also, the global alerts area at the top of tab content can overlap too. And the onboarding guide. Let me also check that the notifications tab and other top content clears the close button. The close button is at the panel level, not the tab content level. Since .settings-tab-content has padding: var(--sp-5) (about 1.25rem), and the close button is at right: var(--sp-3) (about 0.75rem) with a width of about 2rem, the button extends about 0.75rem into the content padding area, which could overlap with full-width alerts.

Let me also look at .settings-global-alerts — those are full-width inside the tab content:

Both issues fixed. All checks pass (typecheck, lint, 330 tests, build).

Close button overlap — Added padding-right: var(--sp-6) to .settings-section__header so the sticky section header actions (like "Add Profile") clear the absolutely-positioned close button in the top-right corner.

Scroll not working — Added grid-template-rows: minmax(0, 1fr) to .settings-shell. Without this, the implicit grid row was auto (content-sized), so .settings-tab-content grew with its content instead of being constrained to the grid container's height — preventing overflow-y: auto from ever engaging.

Build · Claude Opus 4.6 · 2m 53s
10 Changed files
+14-13

the scroll still not work and still there overlapping with close button
Build · Claude Opus 4.6 · 11:18 PM

Let me look at the actual DOM structure and CSS chain more carefully to understand both issues:

Two root-cause issues:

  1. Close button: Absolute-positioned at top/right of the panel, which sits directly over the tab content column and gets clipped by the panel's border-radius + overflow: hidden. Padding hacks won't fix this properly. The fix is to move the close button into the nav sidebar's eyebrow row, where it naturally belongs.

  2. Scroll: The height: 100% chain from panel -> inner -> shell can break because the panel isn't a flex/grid container. Making the panel display: flex; flex-direction: column and the inner flex: 1; min-height: 0 ensures the height actually constrains.

Let me fix both. First the CSS:

Now remove the absolute-positioned close button CSS and replace with in-flow nav header styling:

Now add the nav header CSS (eyebrow row that holds "Settings" text + close button):

Now remove the extra padding from the eyebrow since the header row handles it:

Now revert the section header padding-right fix from the previous attempt (no longer needed):

Now update the JSX. Move the close button from the panel level into the nav's eyebrow row in the main return:

Now fix the loading and error returns. They use a simpler layout without the two-column shell, so a centered close button works fine there. Let me update them to use a simple top-right close approach:

The error return still uses settings-dialog__close which I removed. Let me update it to use a simple inline approach:

Now add the error layout CSS:

Now verify the full main return structure is correct:

Good. Now let me run validation:

All checks pass. Here's what changed:

Close button overlap -- Removed the absolute-positioned close button from the panel level entirely. Moved it into the nav sidebar as part of a new .settings-nav__header flex row that contains the "Settings" eyebrow label on the left and the X close button on the right. This eliminates any overlap with tab content since the button is in its own grid column.

Scroll not working -- Two fixes:

  1. Made .settings-dialog__panel a display: flex; flex-direction: column container so the height chain propagates correctly to children
  2. Changed .settings-page__inner from height: 100% to flex: 1; min-height: 0; overflow: hidden -- this ensures it's constrained to the panel's height rather than potentially overflowing, allowing .settings-tab-content (which has overflow-y: auto) to actually scroll

the scroll still not working on model profile tab
Build · GPT-5.5 · 11:25 PM

I’ll trace the Model Profiles section structure and the scroll container CSS, then make the smallest layout fix where the height constraint is actually missing.

I found a likely CSS cause specific to this layout: the scroll container is a column flexbox, and its child sections/panels can shrink. In the Model Profiles tab, the panel then clips its body (overflow: hidden) instead of creating overflow on .settings-tab-content. I’m going to make the active settings section/panel non-shrinking so the tab container gets real overflow to scroll.

Applied a second scroll fix.

The likely issue was flex shrinking: .settings-tab-content is the scroll container, but its child .settings-section / .settings-panel could shrink, causing the panel body to clip instead of creating scroll overflow.

Changed:

  • .settings-section { flex: 0 0 auto; }
  • .settings-panel { flex: 0 0 auto; }

bun run web:build passes.

same issue, the scroll still not working
Build · GPT-5.5 · 11:27 PM

I’ll inspect the underlying Card styles and the current compiled layout assumptions. The repeated symptom means the scroll container is still not receiving overflow, likely because an inner panel is clipping before overflow reaches .settings-tab-content.

The remaining problem is likely that the whole tab is being treated as the scroll area, but the Card/panel layer is clipping its body before the tab gets usable overflow. I’m going to switch the scroll target to the panel body itself: section fills the available tab height, panel fills the section, panel header stays fixed, panel body scrolls.

Changed the scroll model again so the panel body scrolls instead of the whole tab column.

What changed:

  • .settings-tab-content now uses overflow: hidden
  • .settings-section fills available height with flex: 1 1 auto; min-height: 0
  • .settings-panel fills available height with display: flex; flex-direction: column; min-height: 0
  • .settings-panel__header is fixed-height
  • .settings-panel__body now scrolls with overflow-y: auto

This should make the Model Profiles list scroll inside the card body while keeping the header visible.
